questions.question: Explain the difference between static and dynamic scheduling in OpenMP with appropriate use cases for each.
keyPoints: Static: compile-time assignment, low overhead, best for uniform workloads
Dynamic: runtime assignment, higher overhead, best for variable/unpredictable workloads
Use cases: static for regular matrix operations, dynamic for processing variable-sized tasks

questions.question: What is guided scheduling in OpenMP? Explain how the chunk size is determined in guided scheduling with the formula.
keyPoints: Guided scheduling is dynamic with decreasing chunk sizes
Chunk size = max(chunk, remaining_iterations/num_threads)
Starts large for efficiency, decreases for load balance
Minimum chunk size can be specified

questions.question: Explain the purpose of the schedule(runtime) clause in OpenMP. How can the scheduling policy be specified at runtime?
keyPoints: Defers scheduling decision to runtime
Policy determined by OMP_SCHEDULE environment variable
Format: export OMP_SCHEDULE="policy,chunk"
Can also use omp_set_schedule() function

questions.question: Discuss the trade-off between chunk size and scheduling overhead in dynamic scheduling.
keyPoints: Smaller chunks = better load balance but higher overhead
Larger chunks = lower overhead but potentially worse load balance
Optimal chunk size depends on iteration cost and workload variance
Chunk=1 maximizes balance but has maximum overhead

questions.question: What is auto scheduling in OpenMP and when would you use it?
keyPoints: Compiler and runtime automatically select scheduling strategy
Based on analysis of loop characteristics and runtime behavior
Useful when optimal schedule is difficult to determine manually
Reduces programmer burden but may not always be optimal

questions.question: How does scheduling interact with false sharing in cache-intensive loops?
questions.answer: Larger static chunks reduce false sharing by keeping adjacent iterations on the same thread accessing contiguous memory, improving cache line utilization.
keyPoints: Dynamic scheduling with chunk=1 increases false sharing probability
Static scheduling with aligned large chunks improves data locality
Proper chunk size selection can significantly improve cache performance

questions.question: Given a loop with 10,000 iterations on 8 threads, calculate the iteration assignment for (a) schedule(static), (b) schedule(dynamic,500), (c) schedule(guided,100).
keyPoints: Static: 10000/8 = 1250 iterations per thread (0-1249, 1250-2499, etc.)
Dynamic,500: chunks of 500 assigned as threads become available
Guided: starts ~1250, decreases but never below 100

questions.question: A parallel image processing application processes images of varying sizes using OpenMP. The current implementation uses static scheduling and takes 45 seconds. Profiling reveals significant load imbalance with thread idle times ranging from 2-15 seconds. Suggest improvements to the scheduling strategy, explain your reasoning, and propose a complete solution with expected performance impact.
keyPoints: Analyze load imbalance: variable image sizes cause uneven work distribution
Recommend dynamic or guided scheduling: assign one image at a time for maximum flexibility
Consider hybrid approach: group images by approximate size, use static within groups
Estimate improvement: dynamic may reduce to ~30-35 seconds if overhead is manageable
Alternative: use tasks for irregular workloads (#pragma omp task)
Consider chunk size tuning: start with dynamic,1 then adjust based on profiling

questions.question: Design an optimal OpenMP parallelization strategy for a scientific simulation with the following characteristics: (a) 10,000 iterations, (b) iteration execution time varies from 0.1ms to 10ms based on data dependencies, (c) iterations access large arrays with irregular access patterns, (d) cache efficiency is critical for performance. Justify your choice of scheduling strategy with detailed analysis.
keyPoints: Variable execution time (0.1ms-10ms = 100x variance) strongly suggests dynamic/guided scheduling
For cache efficiency with irregular access: larger chunks improve locality, but need balance
Recommended: schedule(guided, 50-100) - starts large for cache, decreases for balance
Alternative analysis: consider breaking into tasks for complex dependencies
Performance estimates: expect 70-80% efficiency vs 40-50% with static
Trade-off analysis: guided provides middle ground between overhead and load balance
If dependencies allow: consider tiling/blocking first to improve data locality before parallelizing

questions.question: Write and analyze an OpenMP program that demonstrates the performance impact of different scheduling strategies. Include code for static, dynamic, and guided scheduling on a synthetic workload with variable iteration costs. Explain how you would measure and compare the performance.
keyPoints: Create synthetic workload with iteration cost = f(iteration_index) for variability
Implement all three schedules with timing using omp_get_wtime()
Measurement methodology: run multiple iterations, collect statistics (mean, std dev)
Expected results: static fastest for uniform, dynamic/guided better for variable workloads
Analysis should include: execution time, thread idle time, load imbalance metric
Consider: cache effects, barrier synchronization time, scalability
